Jeremiah is sought out to serve a purpose, and the captains ask for his help. In every difficult or uncertain case, we must look to God for direction; and we can still, in faith, pray to be guided by wisdom in our hearts and by Providence. We do not truly desire to know God’s mind if we do not fully resolve to comply with it when we know it. Many promise to do what the Lord requires, hoping their pride will be flattered and their favorite desires spared. Yet something reveals the state of their hearts.

If we want to know the mind of the Lord in uncertain cases, we must wait as well as pray. God is always ready to return in mercy to those he has afflicted; and he never rejects anyone who relies on his promises. He has declared enough to silence even the unfounded fears of his people, which discourage them in the way of duty. Whatever loss or suffering we may fear from obedience is provided for in God’s word; and he will protect and deliver all who trust in him and serve him. It is foolish to abandon our place, especially a holy land, because we encounter trouble in it. And the evils we think to escape through sin, we certainly bring upon ourselves. We can apply this to common troubles of life; and those who think to avoid them by changing their location will find that grievances common to all people will meet them wherever they go. Sinners who deceive God with solemn promises should be rebuked sharply; for their actions speak more plainly than words. We do not know what is good for ourselves; and what we are most fond of, and have our hearts most set upon, often proves hurtful, and sometimes fatal.
